one_liner: API service that retrieves and transcribes YouTube video captions and metadata with optional translation and ASR fallback.

auth_methods.notes: Bearer token in Authorization header (e.g., 'Bearer YOUR_API_KEY') or x-api-key header depending on provider

self_serve_notes: Multiple providers offer self-serve access: youtubetranscript.dev offers 10 free credits monthly; downloadyoutubetranscripts.com provides 10 free credits at signup; youtube-transcript.ai offers keyless fair-use limits; Supadata provides 100 free requests. All require simple sign-up, no credit card for trials.

api_surface.notes: REST API with endpoints for single/batch transcription, job polling, upload management, channel resolution, and credit estimation. Supports both synchronous caption retrieval and async ASR fallback. Multiple format options (timestamps, paragraphs, word-level). V2 and legacy V1 available.

evidence.supports: REST API with Bearer token auth, V2 endpoints for transcribe, batch, jobs, channels. POST /api/v2/transcribe with Authorization header, includes ASR options, webhook support, format options.
REST API authentication via Bearer token (ytk_YOUR_API_KEY). POST /api/v1/transcripts endpoint. Paid plans required (Plus, Pro, Enterprise). Self-serve API key creation from profile page.
10 free credits monthly, credit-based system, 1 request per second rate limit on free plan. Self-serve signup.
Keyless, zero-setup service with fair-use limits. GET requests to https://youtube-transcript.ai/transcript/{VIDEO_ID}.txt without API key.
Existing MCP server implementation for YouTube transcripts using npx deployment. Provides get_transcript, get_timed_transcript, and get_video_info tools.
